translation: I have told you these things so that you may have peace in me. In the world you have affliction; but take courage—I have overcome the world.

alternate_translations.translation: These things I have spoken to you so that you may have peace through me. In the world you will have tribulation; but be of good courage: I have conquered the world.
alternate_translations.notes: “θα ρσεῖτε” can be rendered as “be of good courage,” and “θλῖψιν” often corresponds to “tribulation.” “ἐγὼ νενίκηκα” is perfect tense and can be translated as “I have conquered/overcome,” emphasizing completed victory with present effect.

notes: This passage (from John 16:33) uses contrast language: the disciples will experience pressure/affliction “in the world,” but they should take heart because Jesus has already secured victory “over the world.” The phrase “in me” indicates peace rooted in union with Jesus, not merely obtained by outward circumstances. The punctuation and tone are often rendered as a shift from hardship to encouragement.
